name: Revenue Architecture
headline: Understanding how the business makes money.
oneLineDistillation: How the business makes money, what enables growth, and what blocks it.
whyItMatters: Most GTM mistakes start in the wrong place. Before positioning, before pipeline, before any campaign, the operator has to understand the commercial physics of the business. How revenue is generated, what makes it easier, what restricts it, where the leverage actually sits. Get this wrong and every later move is built on the wrong floor.

whatSitsInside.title: Understand the main business goal
Understand the business model dynamics
whatSitsInside.detail: Transactional, licence, usage, hybrid, B2B2B.

whatSitsInside.title: Identify revenue concentration and customer concentration risk
Identify the growth enablers
whatSitsInside.detail: What makes revenue easier.

whatSitsInside.title: Identify the growth restrictors
whatSitsInside.detail: What blocks revenue.

whatSitsInside.title: Map the unit economics
whatSitsInside.detail: CAC, LTV, payback period, gross margin.

whatSitsInside.title: Map the sales cycle reality
whatSitsInside.detail: Length, decision-maker count, deal size by segment.

strategicAreas: Marketplace vs SaaS vs infrastructure economics
B2B vs B2B2B vs ecosystem models
keyOutputs: Expansion opportunity map and strategic GTM priorities
auditNote: L1 sits before the audit. There is no diagnostic layer feeding it. The reader brings their own commercial physics to the rest of the system.

name: Market Understanding & Positioning
headline: Creating the clearest commercial brief possible.
oneLineDistillation: The brief that the rest of the system runs on.
whyItMatters: Everything downstream runs on the brief. ICP, positioning, messaging, anti-fit, the one-liner. If these are vague, every subsequent system inherits the vagueness. Sharp positioning is the cheapest leverage in GTM, and the easiest thing to leave half-finished.

whatSitsInside.title: Understand or update ICPs from an account and contact point of view
Understand or update positioning
Understand or update the messaging house
Map the market language and all its acronyms
Translate technical complexity into commercial understanding
Define anti-fit
whatSitsInside.detail: Who you do not sell to.

whatSitsInside.title: Build the one-liner and the elevator pitch
Define the category narrative
whatSitsInside.detail: Creator, defender, follower.

caseStudyCategory: Startup GTM Foundations + Infrastructure & Payments GTM

name: Ecosystem & Market Mapping
headline: Mapping the zoo.
oneLineDistillation: The list is the market. The brief becomes a working set of accounts.
whyItMatters: An ICP on a slide is not a market. The list is the market. Until competitors, partners, journalists, events and accounts are mapped, segmented and tiered, the ICP cannot be acted on. This is where the abstract becomes a working set of accounts and contacts the rest of the system can run against.

whatSitsInside.title: Build the TAM and ecosystem map
Map competitors, partners, influencers, journalists, and events
Segment and prioritise ICPs
whatSitsInside.detail: Tier 1, 2, 3 with fit criteria.

whatSitsInside.title: Create structured market databases and lists
Engineer account prioritisation systems
Build the six lists
whatSitsInside.detail: Competitors, partners, ICPs, adjacent excellent companies, events, influencers/journalists/consultants.

whatSitsInside.title: Set up enrichment infrastructure
whatSitsInside.detail: Firmographic, contact waterfall, custom data points.

whatSitsInside.title: Manage data decay against the 22%/year baseline

name: Signals & Market Intelligence
headline: Listening for content appetite and buying appetite.
oneLineDistillation: What the market is saying and when accounts are moving.
whyItMatters: The market is always sending signals. Most companies do not have the infrastructure to hear them. Listening systems do two jobs at once. Content listening tells you what to publish; signal listening tells you when to reach out. Without them, content is broadcast and outbound is guesswork.

whatSitsInside.title: Build social listening systems
Monitor competitor positioning and market narratives
whatSitsInside.detail: Press, blogs, podcasts, LinkedIn, Twitter, Substack, Reddit.

whatSitsInside.title: Track funding, hiring, partnerships, launches, and buying signals
Build trigger-based outreach systems
Connect signals into CRM and outbound workflows
Set up website visitor de-anonymisation
Capture LinkedIn engagement
whatSitsInside.detail: Post engagers, profile visitors.

whatSitsInside.title: Build third-party intent signal capture
whatSitsInside.detail: G2, Bombora, 6sense.

whatSitsInside.title: Score accounts on fit × intent
Build lead-to-account matching
Engineer signal-to-action routing
whatSitsInside.detail: Sub-1-hour SLA.

name: Demand Creation & Relationship GTM
headline: Building prospect delight systems.
oneLineDistillation: When the listening system fires, this is what the prospect meets.
whyItMatters: When the listening system fires, this is what the prospect meets. Every first encounter is either remarkable or wasted. Content as pre-sales infrastructure, diagnostic-first sales, sales rooms, IRL events; these are the surfaces where trust gets built before any pitch lands. Listening without first-touch design is just expensive surveillance.

whatSitsInside.title: Create content as pre-sales infrastructure
whatSitsInside.detail: The silent 80% of buyer research.

whatSitsInside.title: Build event-led and relationship-led GTM motions
Design diagnostic-first sales processes
whatSitsInside.detail: No pitch in the first meeting.

whatSitsInside.title: Build sales enablement assets and sales rooms
whatSitsInside.detail: Per-deal, instrumented, decision-maker visible.

whatSitsInside.title: Run podcast and visibility partnerships

name: Distribution & Signal-Based Activation
headline: Engineering attention, reach, and commercial timing.
oneLineDistillation: Founder, executive and employee distribution; signal-driven outbound on the same logic.
whyItMatters: Reach is a system, not a personality. Founders, executives, ICP-lookalike employees and self-appointed enthusiasts each carry distribution if you give them positioning, content pillars, cadence and a way to operate. Signal-based outbound rides the same logic. The right person says the right thing to the right account at the right moment.

whatSitsInside.title: Build founder and employee advocacy systems
Design LinkedIn and personal brand distribution systems
Activate in order
whatSitsInside.detail: Founders, executive team, ICP-lookalike employees, self-appointed enthusiasts.

whatSitsInside.title: Create outbound campaigns around signals and timing
Engineer customer journeys and deal acceleration systems
Build positioning per LinkedIn profile
whatSitsInside.detail: Four content pillars per person, four posts per week cadence.

whatSitsInside.title: Self-produced content with gamification, or ghostwritten and ghost-commented under delegation
Pre-populate outreach templates connected to signal triggers
GEO across every asset

name: Paid Acquisition & Amplification
headline: Scaling reach through paid distribution.
whyItMatters: Paid is the amplifier, not the engine. Once the listening system, advocacy and outbound are running, paid scales the parts that work and defends the brand searches that come of them. Without the rest of the stack, paid is rented attention.

whatSitsInside.title: LinkedIn
whatSitsInside.detail: Organic-aligned, ABM, retargeting, Thought Leader Ads.

whatSitsInside.detail: Top-of-funnel demand generation, retargeting where ICP is present.

whatSitsInside.detail: Demand gen, demand capture, ABM.

name: Revenue Orchestration & Customer Activation
headline: Aligning teams around customer momentum.
oneLineDistillation: Most pipeline leaks at the seams between teams.
whyItMatters: Most pipeline leaks at the seams between teams, not inside any one team. Marketing-to-sales handoffs, sales-to-CS handoffs, deal-desk discipline, forecast accuracy. These are the seams. Orchestrate them and the same pipeline carries materially more revenue with materially less heroics.

whatSitsInside.title: Coordinate sales, product, marketing, partnerships, and customer success as one revenue motion
Support onboarding and customer activation
Define and govern handoff SLAs across marketing, sales, CS
Document pipeline stages with exit criteria and deal-desk governance
Build forecasting discipline
whatSitsInside.detail: Target 85%+ accuracy.

whatSitsInside.title: Design qualification framework
whatSitsInside.detail: BANT, MEDDIC, MEDDPICC, and enforce in CRM.

whatSitsInside.title: Set up call recording, coaching, and AI insight loops back into pipeline and content
Build win/loss analysis as a quarterly programme with feedback to product and marketing
Build closed-lost reactivation triggered by signal changes
Build buying committee mapping with champion-moved tracking
Build proposal and quote-to-cash automation with discount governance

name: Ecosystem Expansion & Partner Enablement
headline: Turning customers into distribution channels.
oneLineDistillation: The second funnel: where ecosystem businesses leak the most revenue.
whyItMatters: B2B2B and white-label models have a second funnel inside them. The customer signs, then has to sell your product onward. Most companies are not set up for this. The companies that solve it compound, partner growth becomes the dominant revenue line, the original sale becomes a starting point.

whatSitsInside.title: Create partner enablement systems
Build white-label activation systems
whatSitsInside.detail: The customer running your playbook on their customers.

whatSitsInside.title: Build lifecycle and upsell communication systems
Provide 80%-built templates for every touchpoint
whatSitsInside.detail: ICP sizing, messaging house, outbound campaigns, diagnostic session, sales room template, enablement collateral.

whatSitsInside.title: Build renewal pipeline with stage logic and forecasting
Build expansion plays triggered by usage and signals
Set up health scoring, QBRs, and expansion automation
Build lifecycle communications tied to product usage and milestones

name: Commercial Operating Systems & Visibility
headline: Building the GTM operating system.
oneLineDistillation: The meta-layer that turns nine excellent parts into one compounding business.
whyItMatters: Nine excellent parts do not make a system. Visibility, dashboards, automation, AI workflows, GEO, weekly cadence; this is the layer that turns the parts into a compounding business. Without it, you have a collection of tactics. With it, you have a GTM operating system.

whatSitsInside.title: Visibility and analytics across the revenue journey
whatSitsInside.detail: Weekly, monthly, quarterly, half-yearly and annual updates and reports.

Build the operating system between product and market
Engineer trust, attention, and commercial momentum
